name: Bear CRUD
summary: Capabilities for creating, reading, listing, updating, and deleting Bear resources with id/name/age/colour schema, UUIDv7 generation, validation rules for name and age, freeform colour, and cursor-based pagination backed by in-memory storage.

requirements.title: Create Bear
requirements.statement: The system SHALL create a bear record from a client-provided name, age, and colour, assigning a service-generated UUIDv7 identifier and returning the complete record.

acceptance.given: a non-empty, unique name no longer than 100 characters that also satisfies applicable character/pattern restrictions, a non-negative integer age, and a freeform colour
acceptance.outcome: the system stores the bear and returns the record including the generated UUIDv7 identifier

acceptance.given: a create request with an empty name
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a create request with a name that exceeds 100 characters
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a create request with a name that duplicates an existing bear name
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a create request with a name that violates applicable character/pattern restrictions
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a create request with a negative age
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a create request with an age that is not an integer
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

requirements.title: Read Bear
requirements.statement: The system SHALL retrieve a single bear by its UUIDv7 identifier.

acceptance.given: a bear exists with a known UUIDv7 identifier
acceptance.when: a read request is made for that identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system returns the corresponding bear record

acceptance.given: a read request for a UUIDv7 identifier that does not exist
acceptance.outcome: the system indicates that the bear was not found

requirements.title: List Bears
requirements.statement: The system SHALL list all stored bears using cursor-based pagination.

acceptance.given: one or more bears exist in storage
acceptance.outcome: the system returns a paginated result set using cursor-based pagination

acceptance.given: no bears exist
acceptance.outcome: the system returns an empty paginated result set

requirements.title: Update Bear
requirements.statement: The system SHALL update an existing bear, identified by UUIDv7, by fully replacing its mutable fields (name, age, colour).

acceptance.given: an existing bear and a replacement representation that satisfies all creation validation rules
acceptance.outcome: the system overwrites the bear's mutable fields and returns the updated record

acceptance.given: an update request with an invalid age
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the update

acceptance.given: an update request with a duplicate name
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the update

acceptance.given: an update request with an empty name
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the update

acceptance.given: an update request that omits one or more mutable fields
acceptance.outcome: the system applies full replacement semantics and stores exactly the provided values

requirements.title: Delete Bear
requirements.statement: The system SHALL delete a bear by its UUIDv7 identifier.

acceptance.given: an existing bear
acceptance.when: a delete request is made by identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system removes the bear from storage

acceptance.given: a delete request for a UUIDv7 identifier that does not exist
acceptance.outcome: the system indicates that the bear was not found

requirements.title: Bear in-memory persistence
requirements.statement: The system SHALL retain all bear records in volatile, in-memory storage with no persistence across process restarts.

acceptance.given: bears have been created in the service
acceptance.outcome: no bear records remain

requirements.title: In-memory operation latency
requirements.statement: The system SHALL process all CRUD operations with latency consistent with in-memory data access and without reliance on external persistence services.

acceptance.given: bear CRUD operations are executed
acceptance.outcome: responses are produced without reliance on external persistence services and with latency consistent with in-memory access

requirements.title: Local UUIDv7 generation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL generate UUIDv7 identifiers using only local runtime capabilities.

acceptance.given: the service generates a bear identifier
acceptance.outcome: the identifier is produced using only local runtime capabilities without depending on external network services

rules.rule: A Bear id is immutable and is system-assigned as a UUIDv7 value at creation time; clients cannot supply or modify it.
rules.rationale: Guarantees unique, ordered identity without client coordination and prevents identity spoofing or alteration.

rules.rule: A Bear name acts as a unique key across the entire bear collection.
rules.rationale: Prevents duplicate bear records and ambiguous identification.

rules.rule: A Bear age is strictly a non-negative integer; fractional or negative values are prohibited.
rules.rationale: Ensures age is stored as a valid whole number that represents a meaningful duration.

rules.rule: A Bear name must be non-empty, must not exceed 100 characters, and is subject to additional applicable character/pattern restrictions.
rules.rationale: Maintains data quality and consistent naming constraints.

rules.rule: A Bear colour is freeform text with no predefined enumeration, format, or length constraint.
rules.rationale: Allows clients to capture colour values flexibly without constraining the domain model.

rules.rule: Bear updates use full replacement semantics, meaning the request representation entirely supersedes the previous mutable state of the resource.
rules.rationale: Avoids partial-update ambiguity and keeps the state transfer contract explicit.

rules.rule: Bear data persists only in memory for the lifetime of the process; no durable storage or cross-process replication is provided.
rules.rationale: Matches the explicit in-memory store requirement and sets clear durability expectations.
